title: Getting started with Matisse
text: Genes can spliced different ways: certain exons may included transcripts skipped others. called alternative splicing, means one gene can produce multiple protein isoforms different functions. Matisse measures alternative splicing one cell time. cell dataset, calculates PSI value (Percent Spliced ) splicing event care : PSI = 1 — every transcript cell includes exon PSI = 0 — every transcript skips exon PSI = 0.5 — half include, half skip comparing PSI values across cell types, clusters, conditions, can identify populations splice genes differently — much. Matisse sits top existing Seurat workflow. gene expression data, UMAP, cluster labels stay intact; splicing information simply added alongside .

what: A worked example: the PTBP1 splicing switch in mouse cortex
title: Getting started with Matisse
text: One best-studied examples cell-type-specific splicing Ptbp1 exon 9. non-neuronal cells (astrocytes, progenitors), exon included transcripts (high PSI). cells differentiate mature neurons, neuron-specific splicing factor switches : exon 9 skipped almost neuronal transcripts (low PSI). switch functional consequences PTBP1 protein activity downstream splicing regulation. analyse 500 single cells mouse cortex — 300 neurons 200 astrocytes — use Matisse detect switch survey splicing differences across five well-characterised alternative exons. Note: code shows every step analysis. data bundled package, chunks set eval = FALSE. figures shown generated simulated dataset closely mirrors PSI distributions observed published mouse cortex scRNA-seq studies.

title: Getting started with Matisse
text: Starting materials: Seurat object (seu) already processed clustering UMAP junction count table (jxn_counts) STARsolo — one row per cell, one column per exon-exon junction, values = number reads crossing junction cell splice event table (event_df) defining junctions support inclusion versus skipping event interest
code: library(Matisse)  obj <- CreateMatisseObject(   seurat          = seu,   junction_counts = jxn_counts,   event_data      = event_df )

title: Getting started with Matisse
text: cell splicing event, Matisse sums reads inclusion-supporting junctions exclusion-supporting junctions, computes ratio. Cells fewer min_coverage total reads event left missing — enough data call reliable splicing ratio.
code: obj <- CalculatePSI(obj, min_coverage = 5)

title: Getting started with Matisse
text: interpreting results, check cell enough junction data reliable. Per-cell splicing QC metrics split cell type. neurons astrocytes show similar junction detection rates event coverage, indicating balanced data quality across populations. cell types show comparable data quality: nearly cells detect 10 junctions achieve full event coverage. filtering needed , noisier real dataset remove low-quality cells stage:
code: obj <- ComputeIsoformQC(obj) PlotQCMetrics(obj, group_by = "cell_type") # Remove cells with too few detected junctions or too little event coverage obj <- FilterCells(   obj,   min_junctions      = 5,   min_junction_reads = 20,   min_pct_covered    = 10 )  # Remove events that are measurable in too few cells or show no variation obj <- FilterEvents(   obj,   min_cells_covered = 20,   min_psi_variance  = 0.01 )

previous_headings: Step 4 — Visualise the PTBP1 splicing switch
what: Where does the switch happen on the UMAP?
title: Getting started with Matisse
text: Overlay PTBP1 exon 9 PSI value onto cell UMAP. dot cell, coloured PSI value: blue = exon skipped (low PSI), red = exon included (high PSI). UMAP coloured PTBP1 exon 9 PSI. Neurons (left cluster) uniformly blue — exon 9 almost always skipped. Astrocytes (right cluster) uniformly red — exon 9 almost always included. splicing switch perfectly mirrors cell-type boundary. two clusters completely separated splicing state alone — without using gene expression information.
code: PlotPSIUMAP(   obj,   event_id = "PTBP1:SE:chr18:3433647-3436055",   title    = "PTBP1 exon 9 — PSI per cell" )

previous_headings: Step 4 — Visualise the PTBP1 splicing switch
title: Getting started with Matisse
text: Distribution PTBP1 exon 9 PSI values neurons versus astrocytes. Neurons cluster tightly near PSI = 0 (exon nearly always skipped); astrocytes cluster near PSI = 0.8–1.0 (exon nearly always included). near-binary switch characteristic regulated developmental splicing event. Neurons show tight distribution near PSI = 0 (median ~0.08); astrocytes show tight distribution near PSI = 0.82. difference large, consistent, cell-type-specific — exactly signature developmentally regulated splicing switch.
code: PlotPSIViolin(   obj,   event_id = "PTBP1:SE:chr18:3433647-3436055",   group_by = "cell_type",   title    = "PTBP1 exon 9 — PSI by cell type" )

what: Step 5 — Survey splicing across multiple events
title: Getting started with Matisse
text: Use heatmap get overview five events , cells ordered cell type. Rows cells, columns splicing events. PSI heatmap across five alternative exons. Cells ordered cell type (neurons top half, astrocytes bottom half); events hierarchically clustered. PTBP1 NRXN1/MAP2 show opposing directionality — events skipped neurons included astrocytes vice versa. FLNA exon 30 shows difference, confirming pattern event-specific rather global shift. heatmap reveals overall splicing landscape: PTBP1 exon 9: low PSI neurons, high astrocytes (exon skipped neurons) NRXN1 SS4 MAP2 exon 16: high PSI neurons, low astrocytes (neuron-enriched inclusion) FLNA exon 30: difference cell types — useful negative control showing every event regulated MAPT exon 10: mild difference, illustrating splicing changes come spectrum effect sizes
code: PlotPSIHeatmap(obj, group_by = "cell_type", max_cells = 400)

title: Getting started with Matisse
text: junction-count workflow works short-read data (10x Chromium, STARsolo). long-read data — short-read data quantified full-transcript level — Matisse provides second entry point works directly transcript counts.

title: Getting started with Matisse
text: cell event, Matisse sums counts transcripts inclusion set exclusion set computes: PSIc,e=∑inclusion transcript counts∑inclusion counts+∑exclusion countsPSI_{c,e} = \frac{\sum \text{inclusion transcript counts}}                    {\sum \text{inclusion counts} + \sum \text{exclusion counts}} Cells min_coverage total counts event left NA.

title: Getting started with Matisse
text: PSI computed automatically construction — separate CalculatePSI() call needed. junction_counts slot NULL transcript-based objects (junction-level plots available), QC, filtering, visualisation functions work identically.
code: library(Matisse)  # transcript_counts: a matrix (transcripts x cells) — e.g. from Bagpiper # ioe_files: SUPPA2 .ioe output files, one per event type obj <- CreateMatisseObjectFromTranscripts(   seurat            = seu,   transcript_counts = transcript_counts,   ioe_files         = c(     "events_SE.ioe",   # skipped exons     "events_RI.ioe",   # retained introns     "events_SS.ioe"    # alternative splice sites   ),   min_coverage      = 5L )

what: Downstream steps (identical to the junction workflow)
title: Getting started with Matisse
text: Note event IDs: SUPPA2 event IDs use format TYPE:chr:coords:strand (e.g. SE:chr18:100-200:300-400:+). become column names PSI matrix, use format calling PlotPSIUMAP() subsetting obj[, event_id].
code: # QC and filtering obj <- ComputeIsoformQC(obj) PlotQCMetrics(obj, group_by = "cell_type")  obj <- FilterCells(obj, min_pct_covered = 10) obj <- FilterEvents(obj, min_cells_covered = 20, min_psi_variance = 0.01)  # Visualisation — same functions, same arguments PlotPSIUMAP(obj,    event_id = "SE:chr18:3433647-3436055:PTBP1",                     title    = "PTBP1 exon 9 — long-read PSI") PlotPSIViolin(obj,  event_id = "SE:chr18:3433647-3436055:PTBP1",                     group_by = "cell_type") PlotPSIHeatmap(obj, group_by = "cell_type", max_cells = 400)

what: Accessing your data at any point
title: Getting started with Matisse
text: Seurat object metadata remain fully accessible alongside splicing information:
code: # Extract the Seurat object (for any standard Seurat workflow) seu <- GetSeurat(obj)  # Access cell-level metadata with $ obj$cell_type obj$seurat_clusters  # Retrieve the full PSI matrix as a plain table psi_table <- GetPSI(obj)  # Subset to a specific cell type — both gene expression and PSI update together neurons <- obj[obj$cell_type == "Neuron", ]

title: Build a minimal junction event annotation table — BuildSimpleEvents
text: row junctions defines one junction. Events built pairing junctions gene according event_type: simple junction treated event. Inclusion = junction ; exclusion = junctions gene. convenience function exploratory use. production analyses, supply fully annotated event_data table (e.g. rMATS SUPPA2).

what: Calculate PSI matrix from junction counts — CalculatePSI
title: Calculate PSI matrix from junction counts — CalculatePSI
text: Computes Percent Spliced (PSI) matrix splice events defined event_data. cell \(c\) event \(e\):

title: Calculate PSI matrix from junction counts — CalculatePSI
code: CalculatePSI(object, events = NULL, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MatisseObject' CalculatePSI(   object,   events = NULL,   min_coverage = 5L,   na_fill = NA_real_,   verbose = TRUE )  # S4 method for class 'ANY' CalculatePSI(   object,   events,   min_coverage = 5L,   na_fill = NA_real_,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Calculate PSI matrix from junction counts — CalculatePSI
text: object MatisseObject non-NULL junction_counts slot, sparse matrix (cells x junctions). events object matrix: data.frame columns event_id, inclusion_junctions, exclusion_junctions. object MatisseObject defaults GetEventData(object). ... Additional arguments passed method. min_coverage Integer. Minimum total reads per cell per event report PSI value. Default: 5. na_fill Numeric. Replacement low-coverage entries. Default: NA_real_. verbose Logical. Print progress. Default: TRUE.

title: Calculate PSI matrix from junction counts — CalculatePSI
text: MatisseObject (given one) PSI matrix. MatisseObject: input object psi, inclusion_counts, exclusion_counts slots populated. matrix: dense matrix (cells x events) PSI values.

title: Calculate PSI matrix from junction counts — CalculatePSI
text: $$PSI_{c,e} = \frac{\sum \text{inclusion reads}}                        {\sum \text{inclusion reads} +                         \sum \text{exclusion reads}}$$ Entries total coverage (inclusion + exclusion) falls min_coverage set na_fill (default NA).

title: Calculate PSI matrix from junction counts — CalculatePSI
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Build a small toy junction matrix jxn_mat <- Matrix::sparseMatrix(   i = c(1,1,2,2),   j = c(1,2,2,3),   x = c(10, 5, 8, 3),   dims = c(3, 4),   dimnames = list(     paste0("Cell", 1:3),     c("jxn1","jxn2","jxn3","jxn4")   ) ) events <- data.frame(   event_id             = "SE_gene1",   gene_id              = "gene1",   chr                  = "chr1",   strand               = "+",   event_type           = "SE",   inclusion_junctions  = "jxn1;jxn2",   exclusion_junctions  = "jxn3",   stringsAsFactors     = FALSE ) psi_mat <- CalculatePSI(jxn_mat, events, min_coverage = 3) } # }

what: Compute per-cell isoform QC metrics — ComputeIsoformQC
title: Compute per-cell isoform QC metrics — ComputeIsoformQC
text: Calculates panel QC metrics junction count PSI layers stores isoform_metadata slot. Existing columns names overwritten.

title: Compute per-cell isoform QC metrics — ComputeIsoformQC
code: ComputeIsoformQC(object, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MatisseObject' ComputeIsoformQC(object, min_coverage = 5L, verbose = TRUE)

title: Compute per-cell isoform QC metrics — ComputeIsoformQC
text: object MatisseObject. ... Additional arguments (unused). min_coverage Integer. Minimum total reads consider event "covered" computing n_events_covered. Default: 5. verbose Logical. Default: TRUE.

title: Compute per-cell isoform QC metrics — ComputeIsoformQC
text: updated MatisseObject QC columns added isoform metadata. MatisseObject QC columns added isoform_metadata.

title: Compute per-cell isoform QC metrics — ComputeIsoformQC
text: Computed metrics: n_junctions_detected Number junctions least one read. total_junction_reads Total junction read count across junctions. n_events_covered Number splice events PSI \(\geq\) min_coverage (requires PSI calculated first). pct_events_covered Percentage events sufficient coverage. mean_psi Mean PSI across covered events.

title: Create a MatisseObject — CreateMatisseObject
text: primary constructor MatisseObject. Combines Seurat object optional isoform-resolved data layers.

title: Create a MatisseObject — CreateMatisseObject
code: CreateMatisseObject(   seurat,   junction_counts = NULL,   event_data = NULL,   junction_data = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Create a MatisseObject — CreateMatisseObject
text: seurat Seurat object. Required; provides cell barcodes, gene expression, cell-level metadata. junction_counts sparse matrix (dgCMatrix, cells x junctions) raw per-junction read counts. Row names must match colnames(seurat). Default: NULL. event_data data.frame defining splice events. Required columns: event_id, gene_id, chr, strand, event_type, inclusion_junctions (semicolon-separated junction IDs), exclusion_junctions (semicolon-separated junction IDs). Default: NULL (empty table). junction_data data.frame junction annotations. Required columns: junction_id, chr, start, end, strand, gene_id. Default: NULL (empty table). verbose Logical. Print construction progress. Default: TRUE.

title: Create a MatisseObject — CreateMatisseObject
text: MatisseObject.

title: Create a MatisseObject — CreateMatisseObject
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(Seurat) counts <- matrix(rpois(200, 5), nrow = 20,                  dimnames = list(paste0("Gene", 1:20),                                  paste0("Cell", 1:10))) seu <- CreateSeuratObject(counts) obj <- CreateMatisseObject(seurat = seu) obj } # }

what: Create a MatisseObject from transcript-level counts — CreateMatisseObjectFromTranscripts
title: Create a MatisseObject from transcript-level counts — CreateMatisseObjectFromTranscripts
text: Constructs MatisseObject directly transcript--cell count matrix (e.g. Bagpiper transcript-aware quantifiers) mapping transcripts splice events using SUPPA2 IOE annotation files.

title: Create a MatisseObject from transcript-level counts — CreateMatisseObjectFromTranscripts
code: CreateMatisseObjectFromTranscripts(   seurat,   transcript_counts,   ioe_files,   min_coverage = 5L,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Create a MatisseObject from transcript-level counts — CreateMatisseObjectFromTranscripts
text: seurat Seurat object. Provides cell barcodes gene-level metadata. transcript_counts matrix sparse matrix (transcripts x cells) transcript-level counts. Row names must transcript IDs matching IOE files (e.g. GENCODE/Ensembl IDs). Column names must cell barcodes. ioe_files Character vector paths SUPPA2 .ioe files. Multiple files (one per event type: SE, SS, MX, RI, FL) can supplied combined. file must standard four-column SUPPA2 IOE format: seqname, gene_id;event_id, inclusion_transcripts, total_transcripts. min_coverage Integer. Minimum total transcript counts (inclusion + exclusion) per cell per event required report PSI value. Cells threshold receive NA. Default: 5. verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default: TRUE.

title: Create a MatisseObject from transcript-level counts — CreateMatisseObjectFromTranscripts
text: MatisseObject psi, inclusion_counts, exclusion_counts slots populated. junction_counts slot NULL (applicable transcript-based input). event_data slot uses transcript IDs inclusion_junctions exclusion_junctions columns.

title: Create a MatisseObject from transcript-level counts — CreateMatisseObjectFromTranscripts
text: splice event PSI per cell : $$PSI_{c,e} = \frac{\sum \text{inclusion transcript counts}}                        {\sum \text{inclusion transcript counts} +                         \sum \text{exclusion transcript counts}}$$ Cells total count (inclusion + exclusion) falls min_coverage set NA.

title: Create a MatisseObject from transcript-level counts — CreateMatisseObjectFromTranscripts
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Assuming you have: #   - a Seurat object `seu` #   - a transcripts-x-cells matrix `tx_mat` with GENCODE transcript IDs #   - SUPPA2 IOE files for SE and RI events obj <- CreateMatisseObjectFromTranscripts(   seurat            = seu,   transcript_counts = tx_mat,   ioe_files         = c("events_SE.ioe", "events_RI.ioe"),   min_coverage      = 5L ) } # }

what: Filter cells by isoform QC thresholds — FilterCells
title: Filter cells by isoform QC thresholds — FilterCells
text: Removes cells pass specified thresholds columns isoform_metadata slot. Pass named minimum /maximum bounds.

title: Filter cells by isoform QC thresholds — FilterCells
code: FilterCells(object, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MatisseObject' FilterCells(   object,   min_junctions = NULL,   max_junctions = NULL,   min_junction_reads = NULL,   max_junction_reads = NULL,   min_pct_covered = NULL,   custom_filters = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Filter cells by isoform QC thresholds — FilterCells
text: object MatisseObject. ... Named numeric thresholds; see FilterCells. min_junctions Integer. Minimum n_junctions_detected. Default: NULL (filter). max_junctions Integer. Maximum n_junctions_detected. Default: NULL. min_junction_reads Integer. Minimum total_junction_reads. Default: NULL. max_junction_reads Integer. Maximum total_junction_reads. Default: NULL. min_pct_covered Numeric (0–100). Minimum pct_events_covered. Default: NULL. custom_filters Named list two-element numeric vectors c(min, max) applied arbitrary isoform_metadata columns. Use NA one-sided bound, e.g. list(mean_psi = c(0.1, NA)). Default: NULL. verbose Logical. Default: TRUE.

title: Filter cells by isoform QC thresholds — FilterCells
text: filtered MatisseObject. filtered MatisseObject.

what: Filter splice events by coverage or variance — FilterEvents
title: Filter splice events by coverage or variance — FilterEvents
text: Removes events pass minimum coverage variance thresholds. Operates PSI matrix.

title: Filter splice events by coverage or variance — FilterEvents
code: FilterEvents(object, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MatisseObject' FilterEvents(   object,   min_cells_covered = 10L,   min_psi_variance = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Filter splice events by coverage or variance — FilterEvents
text: object MatisseObject non-NULL psi slot. ... Named thresholds; see FilterEvents. min_cells_covered Integer. Minimum number cells event must non-NA PSI value. Default: 10. min_psi_variance Numeric. Minimum variance PSI across covered cells. Filters constitutively spliced (near PSI = 0 1) events. Default: NULL (variance filter). verbose Logical. Default: TRUE.

title: Filter splice events by coverage or variance — FilterEvents
text: filtered MatisseObject. filtered MatisseObject.

what: Find clusters in PSI space — FindPSIClusters
title: Find clusters in PSI space — FindPSIClusters
text: Builds shared-nearest-neighbour (SNN) graph PSI PCA embedding via Seurat::FindNeighbors(), identifies communities using Louvain algorithm via Seurat::FindClusters().  Cluster labels stored psi_clusters object@seurat@meta.data accessible object\$psi_clusters.

title: Find clusters in PSI space — FindPSIClusters
code: FindPSIClusters(object, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MatisseObject' FindPSIClusters(   object,   resolution = 0.8,   dims = 1:20,   reduction = "psi_pca",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Find clusters in PSI space — FindPSIClusters
text: object MatisseObject whose embedded Seurat contains reduction specified reduction. ... Additional arguments (see FindPSIClusters). resolution Numeric. Louvain resolution parameter (higher value → clusters). Default: 0.8. dims Integer vector. PCA dimensions use SNN graph. Default: 1:20. reduction Character. Name input reduction. Default: "psi_pca". verbose Logical. Default: TRUE.

title: Find clusters in PSI space — FindPSIClusters
text: updated MatisseObject psi_clusters metadata. input MatisseObject psi_clusters added object@seurat@meta.data.

title: Find clusters in PSI space — FindPSIClusters
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ obj <- RunPSIPCA(obj, n_pcs = 30) obj <- FindPSIClusters(obj, resolution = 0.5, dims = 1:20) table(obj$psi_clusters) } # }

what: Select highly variable PSI events — FindVariablePSIEvents
title: Select highly variable PSI events — FindVariablePSIEvents
text: Ranks splice events cross-cell variability retains top n_features.  Two ranking methods available: variance Raw per-event variance (NA imputation).  Good default events comparable mean PSI levels. dispersion Coefficient variation (SD / mean), -weights events whose variance high mean near 0 1.  Useful events span wide range mean PSI. Selected events stored object@misc\$variable_psi_events picked automatically RunPSIPCA events = NULL.

title: Select highly variable PSI events — FindVariablePSIEvents
code: FindVariablePSIEvents(   object,   n_features = 2000L,   method = c("variance", "dispersion"),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Select highly variable PSI events — FindVariablePSIEvents
text: object MatisseObject non-NULL psi slot. n_features Integer. Number variable events retain. Default: 2000. method Character. Ranking criterion: "variance" (default) "dispersion" (coefficient variation). verbose Logical. Default: TRUE.

title: Select highly variable PSI events — FindVariablePSIEvents
text: input MatisseObject selected event IDs stored object@misc\$variable_psi_events.

title: Select highly variable PSI events — FindVariablePSIEvents
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ obj <- FindVariablePSIEvents(obj, n_features = 500) obj <- RunPSIPCA(obj)   # uses variable events automatically } # }

what: Matisse: Multi-modal Analysis of Transcript Isoforms in Single-Cell Sequencing Experiments — Matisse-package
title: Matisse: Multi-modal Analysis of Transcript Isoforms in Single-Cell Sequencing Experiments — Matisse-package
text: Matisse provides integrated framework isoform-resolved single-cell RNA-seq analysis, built top Seurat Signac. Key capabilities: MatisseObject — S4 class wraps Seurat object co-stores junction counts, PSI matrices, splice event annotations, keeping gene expression isoform layers synchronised. PSI calculation — CalculatePSI computes per-cell Percent Spliced values raw junction read counts user-supplied auto-generated event annotation table. Isoform QC — ComputeIsoformQC derives per-cell metrics (junction detection rate, event coverage, mean PSI); FilterCells FilterEvents enforce quality thresholds. Visualization — UMAP overlays, violin plots, PSI heatmaps, junction coverage bar charts via consistent ggplot2-based API.

title: Matisse: Multi-modal Analysis of Transcript Isoforms in Single-Cell Sequencing Experiments — Matisse-package
text: Full documentation vignettes available https://avisrilab.github.io/Matisse.

title: Matisse: Multi-modal Analysis of Transcript Isoforms in Single-Cell Sequencing Experiments — Matisse-package
text: Maintainer: k3yavi avisrilab@gmail.com

title: The MatisseObject S4 class — MatisseObject-class
text: show(MatisseObject): Display summary MatisseObject. x[[: Access isoform metadata columns Seurat slots via [[. Checks isoform_metadata first; falls back embedded Seurat object.

title: The MatisseObject S4 class — MatisseObject-class
text: seurat Seurat object carrying gene-level expression, dimensionality reductions, cell metadata. psi sparse matrix (dgCMatrix, cells x events) PSI values \([0, 1]\). NA entries indicate insufficient read coverage. inclusion_counts sparse matrix (dgCMatrix, cells x events) aggregated inclusion-junction read counts per event. exclusion_counts sparse matrix (dgCMatrix, cells x events) aggregated exclusion-junction read counts per event. junction_counts sparse matrix (dgCMatrix, cells x junctions) raw per-junction read counts. event_data data.frame one row per splice event. Required columns: event_id, gene_id, chr, strand, event_type, inclusion_junctions, exclusion_junctions. junction_data data.frame one row per junction. Required columns: junction_id, chr, start, end, strand, gene_id. isoform_metadata data.frame per-cell isoform QC metrics. Rownames correspond cell barcodes. version Character string recording Matisse version used create object. misc Named list user-defined extra data.

what: Run PCA on the PSI matrix — RunPSIPCA
title: Run PCA on the PSI matrix — RunPSIPCA
text: Imputes missing PSI values (uncovered entries) per-event column means, optionally filters low-variance events, runs principal component analysis via stats::prcomp().  result stored DimReduc named "psi_pca" inside embedded Seurat object accessible via SeuratObject::Embeddings(GetSeurat(object), "psi_pca").

title: Run PCA on the PSI matrix — RunPSIPCA
code: RunPSIPCA(object, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MatisseObject' RunPSIPCA(   object,   n_pcs = 30L,   events = NULL,   cells = NULL,   min_variance = NULL,   center = TRUE,   scale = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Run PCA on the PSI matrix — RunPSIPCA
text: object MatisseObject non-NULL psi slot. ... Additional arguments (see RunPSIPCA). n_pcs Integer. Number principal components compute. Default: 30. events Character vector event IDs include. Default: events. cells Character vector cell barcodes include. Default: cells. min_variance Numeric. Retain events whose variance (NA imputation) >= value. Default: NULL (filtering). center Logical. Center PSI matrix PCA. Default: TRUE. scale Logical. Scale event unit variance. Default: FALSE. verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default: TRUE.

title: Run PCA on the PSI matrix — RunPSIPCA
text: updated MatisseObject "psi_pca" reduction. input MatisseObject "psi_pca" added embedded Seurat reductions.

title: Matisse 0.1.0
text: New MatisseObject S4 class wrapping Seurat object isoform-resolved layers: junction counts, PSI matrix, inclusion/exclusion counts, event junction annotation tables, per-cell isoform metadata. CreateMatisseObject(): primary constructor input validation automatic cell-barcode alignment. CalculatePSI(): computes PSI matrices junction count data splice event definitions. Supports MatisseObject bare matrix input. SummarizePSI(): per-event summary statistics (mean, median, sd, coverage). ComputeIsoformQC(): per-cell QC metrics including junction detection rate, total junction reads, event coverage, mean PSI. FilterCells() FilterEvents(): threshold-based filtering informative removal summaries. Visualization: PlotPSIUMAP(), PlotPSIViolin(), PlotPSIHeatmap(), PlotJunctionCoverage(), PlotQCMetrics(). BuildSimpleEvents(): convenience helper generating one-vs-rest event tables junction annotation. MergeMatisse(): concatenate two MatisseObjects cell-prefix deduplication. GitHub Actions workflows R CMD check (multi-OS), pkgdown website deployment, test coverage reporting.
